text.title: LLM Drift Daily Triage Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in daily drift triage with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Skipped-day — triage missed on busy day; gap created in audit trail.
antipattern.symptom: Friday's regression is discovered Monday after customers complain.
antipattern.root-cause: Ritual not on-call-driven; relies on volunteer attention.
antipattern.fix: Wire triage into on-call rotation with PagerDuty default-assignee fallback (rule r3).

antipattern.description: Eyeball-only — owner glances at a dashboard, no report written.
antipattern.symptom: Cannot reconstruct what was seen or decided a week later; same regression diagnosed twice.
antipattern.root-cause: "Report" felt like overhead; runner template did not exist.
antipattern.fix: Runner produces triage-report.json automatically; owner only adds decision + follow_up (rule r1).

antipattern.description: No escalation path — every delta is "continue" because there is no defined next action.
antipattern.symptom: Real regressions sit in production until a senior engineer happens to notice.
antipattern.fix: Pre-defined per-metric thresholds + per-decision channel (rule r4).

antipattern.description: Single-metric tunnel — only eval score watched; refusal-rate and cost ignored.
antipattern.symptom: Eval looks fine; refusal-rate quietly climbs from 8% to 22% and customer-satisfaction tanks.
antipattern.root-cause: The single-metric was "easy to chart"; the other two require infra.
antipattern.fix: Three-metric report mandated; one runner emits all three (rule r2).

antipattern.description: Retro edit — yesterday's report rewritten after the regression revealed itself.
antipattern.symptom: Audit trail says everything was fine; incident postmortem cannot trust historical reports.
antipattern.root-cause: Reports stored as mutable doc instead of append-only log.
antipattern.fix: Append-only log (git-backed or immutable storage); corrections go in new entry (rule r6).
